title: struthanthus terniflorus
intro: Struthanthus terniflorus, also known as the "Indian pipe" or "ghost orchid," is a rare, non-photosynthetic plant that obtains its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of certain tree species. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in reducing swelling and fighting infections. The health benefits of Struthanthus terniflorus may include its ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea or infused in oil, but more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
